title: Living in Boulder vlog 3 - 18 - 2017: Bike Racer Levels. Downhill.

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs about working on his 'bike race levels' website project, which categorizes race results by time and distance. He discusses the tedious process of organizing race data (including Race Across America results), debates how to sort team vs individual categories, and mentions building a downhill level page where he ranks 8th. He's tired from staying up all night coding.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.topic: organizing team vs individual race data
stances.position: ultimately decided to keep all teams together rather than split by team size, because that was more consistent
sees it as big, complex, and good preparation for future ambitious data projects
wants to eventually build an elaborate economic map/chart of the American economy using similar methods

claims.subject: race cross-American
claims.claim: I even went back to the B.C. decades, starting with the 2,700s and going down to the 250s, which get a lot bigger after the 270s.
